creator: jhale@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Zsolt Udvari from comment #4)
I'm a member of the kde@ team which is responsible for Qt, so there isn't much point in reassigning this right now. I have verified that the Qt 5.15.x versions of the packages you are missing are available for FreeBSD 12.x amd64 and i386 in the 2020Q4 branch. I'm assuming that is what you are using, you haven't said.

If you are using a version of FreeBSD older than 12.x, then we are not providing packages because Qt 5.15.x has dropped support for OpenSSL 1.0. From the release notes:

This version of Qt drops support for OpenSSL 1.0 -- this means that there
won't be any binary packages for Qt5 provided by the FreeBSD package builders
for FreeBSD 11.x anymore -- and the same for *all* the ports depending on
net/qt5-network [3]. If you cannot upgrade to a more recent FreeBSD
version (12.x, 13.x), you will need to build Qt5 from ports while switching
to an SSL implementation from ports.
